---
title: "Generate a QC report"
description: "Standalone quality-control report."
categories: [openpipeline_qc]
order: 50
---

[`openpipeline_qc`]{.pkg-tag .pkg-openpipeline_qc}<span class="swatch openpipeline_qc"></span>

The **`openpipeline_qc`** package produces a standalone HTML quality-control
report from an ingested `.h5mu`, so you can inspect count distributions,
mitochondrial fractions, and doublet signals before committing to
[filter thresholds](process-filter.qmd).

## Run the report

`generate_qc_report` detects the ingestion method, computes QC metrics, and
renders a self-contained HTML report:

```bash
nextflow run openpipelines-bio/openpipeline_qc \
  -r v0.3.0 -latest -profile docker \
  -main-script target/nextflow/workflows/generate_qc_report/main.nf \
  --id cohort_01 \
  --input cohort_01.h5mu \
  --output qc_report.html \
  --publish_dir results/
```

Under the hood the package also exposes `ingestion_qc/detect_ingestion_method`
and `ingestion_qc/generate_html` as individual components, in case you want to
compute metrics and render the report as separate steps.

:::{.callout-note}
`openpipeline_qc` is a separate package pinning core `openpipeline` plus
craftbox. Check the [reference](../reference/index.qmd) (filter by
`openpipeline_qc`) for the exact argument list, and its Viash Hub page for run
instructions.
:::

**Next:** [Process & filter samples →](process-filter.qmd) ·
[QC reference →](../reference/index.qmd)
